samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Converts parallel data to serial format.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal for serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[2:0]">Counter for serial output.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" type="sequential_logic">Valid signal for serial output.</reg>
        <reg id="data" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">Shift register for parallel data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module parallel2serial converts parallel data to serial format. 
    It has 4-bit parallel data input (d) and serial data output (dout). 
    The valid signal (valid_out) indicates the availability of serial output.
    The module uses a counter (cnt) to control the serial output. 
    When the counter reaches 3, the valid signal is set to 1 and the counter is reset to 0. 
    Otherwise, the counter is incremented by 1 and the valid signal is set to 0.
    The data register (data) is used to store the parallel data. 
    When the counter reaches 3, the data register is loaded with the parallel data. 
    Otherwise, the data register is shifted left by 1 bit.
    The most significant bit of the data register is assigned to the serial output (dout).
    </implementation>
</module>
